name: SWFL — News daily (Flow 4, Tier 1 cold storage)

parameters.query: INSERT INTO data_lake._tier1_inventory (id, bucket, path, vintage, byte_size, pack_id, source_url, updated_at) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4::date, $5::bigint, NULL, NULL, now()) ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET byte_size = EXCLUDED.byte_size, vintage = EXCLUDED.vintage, updated_at = now();

options.queryReplacement: ={{ $node['Collect + dedupe + build NDJSON'].json.full_id }},{{ $node['Collect + dedupe + build NDJSON'].json.bucket }},{{ $node['Collect + dedupe + build NDJSON'].json.path }},{{ $node['Collect + dedupe + build NDJSON'].json.vintage }},{{ $node['Collect + dedupe + build NDJSON'].json.byte_size }}

meta._authoring_note: Draft. Pre-flight: (1) Replace YOUR_PROJECT_REF in the Supabase Storage PUT URL with your actual Supabase project ref (e.g. `abc123xyz.supabase.co` → use `abc123xyz`). (2) Confirm lake-tier1 bucket exists in Supabase Storage (created when the data-tier policy was adopted). (3) pack_id is NULL on the inventory row — no consumer yet, per the plan's Tier 1 cold-storage gate. Promotion to Tier 2 happens in a future PR that lands the consuming brain. (4) The Firecrawl Batch Scrape node mentioned in the plan was substituted for parallel /v2/scrape calls — same outcome, doesn't depend on a community-node operation name.
